methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Swift Beat is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Eric Schmidt that has raised $50M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 peers: 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private-stage businesses in the same sector, including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods anchor the assessment. The stage-progression approach applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $50M total raised as a proxy baseline, yielding $100M. The peer-median method examines 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $125M to $3.0B; the median across this set is $1.0B, represented by companies including Castelion and Defense Unicorns at $1.0B, Code Metal at $1.2B, and Harmattan AI at $1.4B.

These two methods triangulate to a valuation range of $85M to $1.1B, with a median of $550M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around Swift Beat's revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and technology differentiation within a sector where peer valuations vary substantially by stage and market traction. The stage-progression method suggests a conservative floor near $85M, while the peer set's upper quartile supports valuations approaching $1.1B conditional on execution metrics and customer acquisition velocity.
